title: Air Astana, Somon Air, and Uzbekistan Airways Order New Boeing Jets

Boeing has announced new aircraft orders from three Central Asian airlines, Air Astana, Somon Air, and Uzbekistan Airways.  All three announcements were made during the C5+1 Summit, commemorating the 10th anniversary of the diplomatic platform linking the United States with the five Central Asian nations. Uzbekistan Airways Expands 787 Dreamliner Fleet Uzbekistan Airways has firmed an order for eight Boeing 787 Dreamliners, increasing its total order book for the type to 22 aircraft. The signing ceremony took place at the […]

title: Airlink continues expansion across southern Africa
contentSnippet:
Airlink (4Z/LNK) is one of South Africa’s leading airlines, based at Cape Town International Airport and Johannesburg OR Tambo International Airport, the country’s main cities, operating a fleet of narrow-body aircraft. SA Airlink The airline was founded in 1992 and began operations in 1995 under the name SA Airlink. That same year, it joined the […]

title: Loganair Strengthens Commitment to Channel Islands Connectivity
contentSnippet:
Loganair {LM/LOG) has reaffirmed its commitment to maintaining vital air links to the Channel Islands, following the collapse of Blue Islands earlier this month. The regional carrier stepped in swiftly after Blue Islands ceased operations on 14 November, launching replacement services just two days later to minimise disruption for travellers.  [read more]
The post Loganair Strengthens Commitment to Channel Islands Connectivity appeared first on UK Aviation News.

title: Why IndiGo Are Cancelling Flights All Across India
contentSnippet:
India’s largest airline IndiGo (6E-IGO) has cancelled over 900 flights in just four days, causing an uproar among travellers. We break down what has happened, and explain why passengers can expect the turbulence to continue for weeks—if not months. Why are IndiGo cancelling flights? title: IT issue suspends flights at Edinburgh Airport
contentSnippet:
Flights in and out of Scotland’s busiest airport were suspended this morning after an IT issue affected Air Traffic Control. Air Traffic services at Edinburgh Airport (EDI/EGPH) are provided by ANSL not the UK’s main provider, NATS which is why disruption is limited to one airport. Several flights were due  [read more]
